rationale: The audience overlap is strong — casual women 25-55 are the same demo that engages with jigsaw "satisfaction" hooks — but the mechanical mismatch is severe: dragging a flat 2D puzzle piece into a frame misrepresents Marble Sort's physics-tap + conveyor + color-bucket loop, risking misleading-ad complaints and bad D1 retention. Palette is also off (saturated blue/magenta vs. soft lavender/orange pastel). Only worth testing as a 3-second hook if the back half of the creative cuts hard to authentic marble-sorting gameplay; otherwise pass given the thin cluster (1 ad, 1 advertiser).
